title: Meet Karol G's Ex-Boyfriend, Feid, A Reggaeton Singer And Songwriter, Has An Alter Ego Called Ferxxo - BollywoodShaadis
content:
By
			
				Avispita Saha			

Last Updated: 
Apr 28, 2026 | 02:43:27 IST Karol G, born as Carolina Giraldo Navarro, is a renowned Colombian singer and songwriter. She has established herself as one of the most influential reggaeton and urban pop artists. She has won several accolades for her music, including a Grammy, eight Latin Grammy Awards, and five Billboard Music Awards. Some of her most notable songs include Location, Bichota, Dios Mio! and Tusa. At present, Karol G is in the limelight after confirming three new dates for Madrid, Barcelona, and Seville, which will be a part of her global Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour. The tickets went on presale today, April 27, 2026. As she continues to grab headlines for her professional life, let us introduce you to Karol G's ex-boyfriend, Feid. Solomon Villada Hoyos, who goes by the name of Feid, was born in Medellin, Colombia. For those unaware, this is the same city that his ex-girlfriend, Karol, was born in. According to Billboard, he began pursuing songwriting after discovering his passion for performing in the seventh grade. While Karol and Feid knew each other because they were from the same city, the duo did not keep in touch until they reconnected in 2021. Speaking to Rolling Stone in 2023, Karol said, “There was a long time when we didn't speak to each other, and I barely knew anything about him.” As someone who is proud of his roots, Feid often incorporates Medellin culture, style, and even slang into his music and performances. Talking about the same to People in 2021, he said, “Everything that inspires my music — my muse if you will — is and always has been my culture and everything that revolves around that.” Apparently, his classic uniform, which includes basketball shorts, an oversized T-shirt, sneakers, sunglasses, and a hat, represents the “nea” subculture in Medellin. Similar to Karol, Feid is also a reggaeton singer and songwriter. For those unaware, reggaeton is a genre with roots in Puerto Rico. It blends together reggae music with hip-hop influences. When discussing the authenticity required to perform the genre, Feid told NPR, “ For [Puerto Rican fans], reggaeton is not pop music. It's music from the streets, music from people who run the streets for real.” He said that there, one cannot be a poser and has to be real with people. After this, it is up to them if they want to connect with someone or not. As we mentioned previously, although Karol and Feid knew each other from Medellin, they did not have the opportunity to truly connect until 2021. This is when Feid opened up for his ex-girlfriend's Bichota tour, which had stops across the US and Puerto Rico. Karol told Billboard in 2021 that she loved how Feid was from her country and her city and expressed her gratitude for him and his team for joining her tour. It is worth noting that in her Netflix documentary, Karol G: Tomorrow Was Beautiful, she shared how she fell in love with Feid while they were touring. She said that without even realising it at first, she began writing love songs again about happiness. After releasing a few singles in his early 20s, Feid began writing songs for other artists. According to a report by People, he had written a song for himself called Robarte Hoy, however, when Colombian artist, Shako asked whether he could record the song himself, Feid agreed. Feid's big break came sometime later when he wrote a song titled Secretos for Reykon. After catching the eye of J Balvin with this song, the duo collaborated on his 2016 chart-topper, Ginza. Following this, Feid has also worked with Christina Aguilera and Micky Jam. In 2023, he told NPR: “A lot of people know me as a songwriter, but it wasn't my motivation. It's not like I was a songwriter and now I'm an artist. I was an artist that had to be a songwriter.” When Feid was touring with Karol G in 2021, the duo collaborated on a song called FRIKI. The son was released in December of the same year. In the teaser for the music video, released one week after the song dropped, Karol and Feid were seen dancing together while performing the song in Colombia. In 2023, Karol revealed to Rolling Stone that she and Feid have another collaboration in the works. At the time, she was working on Verano Rosa, which Feid had planned to jump on. Because the song is about heartbreak, she's said that he told her, “I know you've thought of thousands of people and no one fits but…are you gonna let me get on that song?” While he primarily goes by the stage name, Feid, he also has an alter ego on stage called Ferxxo. According to Billboard, despite launching his career in 2010, Feid adopted his alternate persona only in 2020, which clearly has more Medallo influences. According to the singer, he created this persona to pay homage to his Medellin roots and to show a more authentic side of himself. He said: “At that moment, I opened the coolest door that I’ve ever opened, which was finding my identity and introducing El Ferxxo. It took me a long time to realise that this was what I had to do to really, really connect with people.” What are your thoughts on Karol G's ex-boyfriend, Feid? Let us know. title: Governor Bryan Signs Executive Order Implementing New Pay Plan for Non-Union Classified Government Employees - vi.gov
content: Executive order aligns the government’s non-union GS pay plan with Act No. 8995, which raised the minimum salary for full-time government employees from $27,040 to $35,000 U.S. VIRGIN ISLANDS — Governor Albert Bryan Jr. has signed Executive Order No. 545-2026, implementing a new General Schedule pay plan for non-union classified employees in the Executive Branch of the Government of the Virgin Islands. The executive order is necessary to bring the government’s non-union classified pay structure into compliance with Act No. 8995, which increased the minimum annual salary for full-time employees of the Government of the Virgin Islands, its semi-autonomous agencies, and independent instrumentalities from $27,040 to $35,000. Governor Bryan said the executive order reflects his administration’s responsibility to follow the law while implementing the mandate in a structured and fiscally responsible way. “I have been clear from the beginning that Virgin Islands workers deserve better pay,” Governor Bryan said. “I have also been clear that we cannot govern by hope alone. A pay increase of this magnitude must be implemented with a plan, with accurate data, and with the discipline to make sure we do not weaken the very government services our people depend on. This executive order puts that structure in place for non-union classified employees and ensures that the law is carried out in an orderly and responsible manner.” Under Executive Order No. 545-2026, all non-union classified employees covered by the order will be placed into the updated GS pay plan beginning October 1, 2026, for Fiscal Year 2027. Employees will not be placed below their current salary and will receive a one-step increase within their assigned pay grade. The executive order also provides for additional one-step increases in Fiscal Years 2029, 2031, 2033, 2035, and 2037, subject to the structure of the new GS pay plan. Once an employee reaches the final step in a grade, no additional step increase will be granted under that grade. The Fiscal Year 2027 Budget Proposal submitted to the Legislature includes funding for the initial placement of employees into the new GS pay plan and the first one-step increase. The Office of Management and Budget will include the scheduled future step increases in the appropriate fiscal year budgets. Governor Bryan said the action is part of the administration’s broader effort to balance fair compensation with the long-term financial stability of the Government of the Virgin Islands. “Fair pay and fiscal responsibility are not competing goals,” Governor Bryan said. “The right way to support workers is to make sure compensation reform is sustainable, properly funded, and does not create new obligations the government cannot meet. We have spent years restoring financial discipline, paying down old obligations, issuing tax refunds, addressing retroactive wages, and rebuilding public trust. We are not going back to a time when government made promises without a plan to pay for them.” The executive order applies only to non-union classified employees in the Executive Branch. It does not authorize pay increases for employees represented by a labor organization or employees in the exempt service of the Government of the Virgin Islands. Executive Order No. 545-2026 takes effect October 1, 2026, and remains in effect unless amended or repealed by a subsequent executive order. © 2026 Government of the United States Virgin Islands. © 2026 Government of the United States Virgin Islands

title: Ryan Castro Delivers A Standout Surprise Set At Coachella During Karol G's Weekend 2 Show - The Garnette Report
content: During Weekend 2 of the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival global superstar Ryan Castro was brought out by Karol G as a surprise guest, delivering one of the most defining performances of the night. During his dedicated segment, Castro took over the stage with a high-energy, three-song Caribbean-driven medley. This includes songs such as Sanka, Ba Ba Bad Remix, and Tonto. Castro surprised the crowd even more with fellow artist J Balvin joining him on stage for a standout moment. Castro’s performance pulled the fans directly into the action. Set in a water-filled pit in the middle of the crowd, amplifying the artist’s Caribbean-rooted sound in the festival’s most immersive moment. Along with this fan interaction, Castro’s appearance spotlighted key records from Sende and Hopi Sende along with music from his upcoming collaborative album with J Balvin titled Omerta, set to release on May 7. Known as a global reggaeton and dancehall artist, Castro has bridged culture, sports, and entrepreneurship throughout his career. In 2024, he released his RIAA 2x Platinum-certified debut album El Cantante Del Ghetto. The album delivered the global and viral anthem El Ritmo Que Nos Une for Colombia’s National Soccer Team. Castro then became the first Colombian artist to acquire equity in a professional sports team with the Paisas Basketball Club based in his hometown of Medellín. His momentum reaches a new level with his hit single La Villa from his third album Hopi Sende. The single peaked at #31 on Spotify’s global chart and reached #1 across Latin America. Ryan Castro is now building on this momentum as he prepares for his largest hometown stadium show at Medellín’s Atanasio Girardot on April 25 where he sold out 45,000 tickets in under two hours. title: Volleyball Federation Warns, Fines Ahn Hye-jin Over Drunk Driving - 조선일보
content: Controversy as Federation Cites Low BAC, Repentance, and Existing Penalties in Leniency Decision [OSEN=Lee Hoo-kwang, journalist] The first drunk driving scandal in the V-League women's division. Why did the Korean Volleyball Federation (KOVO) only issue a warning and a fine without a suspension? The Korean Volleyball Federation held a disciplinary committee meeting regarding Ahn Hye-jin's drunk driving at the federation's conference room in the morning of the 27th. Ahn Hye-jin was caught driving under the influence with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.032%, which is subject to license suspension, on the morning of the 16th. The federation confirmed the details of Ahn's drunk driving from her former team, GS Caltex, on the 17th, and held a disciplinary committee on the 27th to give Ahn an opportunity to explain. Ahn visited the federation's office in Sangam-dong, Mapo-gu, Seoul, with her lawyer on the 27th to make her statement. According to the federation, the disciplinary committee confirmed that Ahn violated the Road Traffic Act (drunk driving) after listening to her statement. After reviewing and discussing the case, they issued Ahn a severe warning and a fine of 5,000,000 Korean won without a suspension. According to Article 10, Clause 1 of the federation's disciplinary regulations, penalties for drunk driving can range from a warning to expulsion, and fines must be at least 5,000,000 Korean won. Ahn Hye-jin, who graduated from Gangneung Girls' High School and was selected third in the first round by GS Caltex in the 2016-2017 rookie draft, played as the starting setter during the 2025-2026 season, leading her team to their first championship final victory in five years. GS Caltex, which ranked third in the regular league, advanced through the semifinals and playoffs to the championship final, where they defeated the first-place Korea Expressway Corporation with three straight wins, and Ahn was at the center of this miracle. After the season, Ahn obtained FA status. She was expected to secure a lucrative contract with the title of championship setter, but she committed an irreversible mistake, kicking away her own fortune. Ahn was caught driving under the influence on the 16th and was investigated by the police. The next day, she posted a handwritten apology on her personal SNS. GS Caltex also issued an official apology and bowed to volleyball fans regarding the incident. Ahn became the first player in the V-League women's division to be caught for drunk driving, earning her the disgrace. The federation explained the background of the disciplinary controversy, citing five main reasons. The disciplinary committee acknowledged that violating the Road Traffic Act (drunk driving) is a serious antisocial act and should be severely punished. However, they noted that her blood alcohol level was relatively low at 0.032%, she voluntarily reported the incident to her team and the federation immediately after the accident, and she deeply regretted her mistake and showed repentance. They added that they considered the fact that she effectively received a one-year suspension due to failing to secure an FA contract and her exclusion from the national team. Ahn failed to secure an FA contract during this offseason and will not be able to play in the upcoming 2026-2027 season. It was reported that she did not receive any contract offers from any team, including her former team GS Caltex. Ahn has also been excluded from the national team. Although head coach Cha Sang-hyun included the championship setter Ahn in the 18-member squad summoned to the Jincheon Athlete's Village on the 16th, her inclusion became void due to the drunk driving incident. According to the Korea Volleyball Association, individuals who receive a fine of 5,000,000 Korean won or more within three years or a fine of less than 5,000,000 Korean won within two years for drunk driving cannot be selected for the national team. Although the police investigation is still ongoing, since Ahn's drunk driving was confirmed, she will be ineligible for national team selection for at least the next two years. Wearing a black suit, Ahn, who attended the federation on that day, bowed her head and said, "I am sorry for causing you worry and concern. I sincerely apologize to the fans and all related parties. I promise this will never happen again. I am sorry."

title: KOVO issues warning and fine after Ahn Hye-jin drunk driving case - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
content: V League women's division's first drunk driving scandal. Why did the Korea Volleyball Federation (KOVO) issue only a warning and a fine without suspending eligibility? The Korea Volleyball Federation (KOVO) held a disciplinary committee meeting regarding Ahn Hye-jin's drunk driving at the federation's main conference room on the morning of the 27th. Ahn Hye-jin was caught for drunk driving on the morning of the 16th with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.032%, at the level requiring a license suspension. Accordingly, on the 17th the federation confirmed the details of Ahn Hye-jin's drunk driving from Ahn's former club, GS Caltex, and on the 27th held a disciplinary committee meeting to give Ahn Hye-jin an opportunity to explain. According to the federation, the disciplinary committee listened to Ahn Hye-jin's statement and explanations and confirmed that there had been a violation of the Road Traffic Act (drunk driving). After reviewing and discussing the matter, they imposed a stern warning and a fine of 5 million won on Ahn Hye-jin without suspending her eligibility. Under Article 10, Paragraph 1 of the federation's disciplinary regulations, punishments for drunk driving can range from a warning up to expulsion, and fines must be imposed at no less than 5 million won. Ahn Hye-jin, who graduated from Gangneung Girls' High School and was selected by GS Caltex in the first round with the third pick of the 2016-2017 rookie draft, played as the starting setter in the 2025-2026 season and led her team to the championship for the first time in five years. GS Caltex finished third in the regular season, advanced through the quarterfinals and semifinals, and pulled off a miracle by sweeping first-place Korea Expressway Corporation (KEC) 3-0 in the championship series, with Ahn Hye-jin at the center of that effort. After the season Ahn Hye-jin became a free agent. Although a big contract was expected with the title of championship setter, she committed an irreparable offense and undermined her own prospects. Ahn Hye-jin was caught by police for drunk driving on the 16th and was investigated, and the next day she posted a handwritten apology on her personal social media. GS Caltex also issued an official apology for the incident and bowed to volleyball fans. Ahn Hye-jin earned the dishonor of being the first player since the launch of the V League women's division to be caught drunk driving. The federation explained the backstory of the controversy over the severity of the punishment by citing five main reasons. The disciplinary committee said that although a violation of the Road Traffic Act (drunk driving) is a serious antisocial act warranting heavy punishment, Ahn's blood alcohol concentration was relatively low at 0.032%, she voluntarily reported to the club and the federation immediately after the incident, and the committee judged that she deeply repents and is reflecting on her wrongdoing. They added that they also took into consideration that she effectively received a year-long suspension by not securing an FA contract and that she was excluded from the national team. It was reported that Ahn Hye-jin did not receive any contract offers from any clubs, including her original club GS Caltex. Ahn Hye-jin has also been excluded from the national team. Although head coach Cha Sang-hyun included championship setter Ahn Hye-jin in the 18-player roster to be assembled at the Jinchon training center on the 16th, the drunk driving incident meant the national team jersey would not happen. According to the Korea Volleyball Association, anyone sentenced to a fine of 500,000 won or more within three years for drunk driving, or anyone sentenced to a fine of less than 500,000 won within two years, cannot become a national team member. title: Lady Hatchets run at GS - Washington Times Herald
content: Thunderstorms, some strong early, then cloudy skies after midnight. Damaging winds, large hail and possibly a tornado with some storms. Low 58F. Winds SW at 15 to 25 mph. Winds SW at 15 to 25 mph. Chance of rain 100%. 1 to 2 inches of rain expected. FORT BRANCH – The Lady Hatchets Track and Field team competed in the Gibson Southern Invitation Friday night finishing 5th overall out of 9 teams. Scoring was as follows for the girls’ side: Gibson Southern 172.5, Princeton 102, Heritage Hills 88, Mt. Vernon 81, Washington 75.5, South Knox 41.5, South Spencer 40, Evansville Christian 10.5, Signature High 2. All three of our relay teams marked their best times of the season. The goal of the relays are for all four athletes to have an on night on the same night and it finally clicked for the 4x8 and 4x4 teams knocking their times back to where we like to see them. The 4x8 team of Ally Decker, Adara Rainey, Genesis Gomez and Ayla Owens got their time back down to 10:35, a season best and tying their career best. The 4x4 relay team also had a standout night tying their career best time of 4:15 with the team of Genesis Gomez, Lexi Nalin, Ayla Owens and Adara Rainey that got them a second place finish. Rounding out the relay teams was a fifth place finish for the 4x1 team with Haylee Thompson, Leah Owens, Lexi Nalin and Ava Hill. Javascript is required for you to be able to read premium content. Please enable it in your browser settings. Two sophomores had the top finishes for the Lady Hatchets by bringing home first place medals in their event. Adara Rainey finished first in the 400m dash, while Leah Owens cleared 9’0 in pole vault for a first place finish. Rainey also finished seventh in the 200m dash in 28.49 and was part of both 4x8 and 4x4 season best times. Senior, Ayla Owens had second place finishes in the 1600m run with a career best in 5:48.26 and finished second in pole vault clearing 8’6” while also helping two relay teams accomplish season bests. Ava Hill, sophomore, continues to be at the top of the scoring for the Hatchets as well. She tied her career best in high jump clearing 4’6” for a fourth place finish tie. Hill also finished the 300m hurdle race in fourth place with a time of 52.24. Gomez had a sixth place finish in the 800m run in 2:43.2 where she continues to be a solid force for the team. title: BMW F 450 GS image gallery - Autocar India
content: BMW F 450 GS has been launched in India at a price of Rs 4.70 lakh Powered by a 420cc, parallel-twin engine that produces 48hp and 43Nm The F 450 GS’s gets a KYB upside-down fork and a KYB monoshock Available in three variants - Base, Exclusive and GS Trophy The Base and Exclusive are only available in Cosmic Black shade The GS Trophy variant is the only one available in the BMW tricolour BMW has launched the highly anticipated production version of the F 450 GS at EICMA 2025. With the G 310 GS discontinued, this adventure bike serves as an entry point into BMW’s GS line-up and will be made in India by TVS. The F 450 GS has a kerb weight of 178kg, gets quad-LED DRLs (daytime running lamps) inspired by the R 1300 GS, a single disc brake at the front and a golden-coloured USD fork. Its 420cc twin-cylinder engine produces 48hp at 8,750 rpm and a peak torque of 43Nm at 6,750 rpm.

title: Check Into Summer With Burberry X Hunza G's Swimwear Capsule | Grazia India - Grazia India
content: Burberry teams up with Hunza G for a lido-inspired swimwear capsule Celebrating the British summer and its quintessential culture of open-air pools that come alive with families, friends and neighbours every hot season, Burberry collaborates with British brand Hunza G on a new swimwear capsule launching in summer 2026. The house launches a High Summer 2026 campaign which features a film and portfolio of images capturing life at the lido. “A lido holds a particular kind of nostalgia for the British,” says Daniel Lee, Burberry’s Chief Creative Officer, adding, “The moment the sun comes out, we make the most of the weather. We wanted to bring to life a warm summer’s day spent in and around the water’s edge with friends.” With a shared commitment between both British brands for material innovation, the collection is built on signature Hunza G silhouettes from its Original Crinkle™ ultra-stretch fabric in iconic Burberry colours with Burberry Check trims. Celebrating the launch, photographer Ryan McGinley captures Simone Ashley and Alva Claire in a series of portraits, wearing pieces from the swimwear capsule in picturesque beach settings. The collection features the Burberry Check in heritage-inspired sand beige and pastel shades of aubergine purple and cornflower blue. Expect the Burberry Check bikini, matching swimsuits, festival tank tops, poolside separates like tops, skirts and shirts and cover-up dresses in a colour palette of black, white, metallic cocoa and red. Ruffled trims, ties at the neckline capture the effortless feel of the collection. Each piece also comes with a matching scrunchie. For men, it offers collared shirts, swim shorts, a co-ord set and the classic cotton piqué polo shirt. Lightweight hooded jackets ideal for cooler evenings are another addition. Made for the beach, the shoe collection includes Burberry Check slides, Pavilion sandals, elevated sandals, and new Knight Runner sneakers. Accessories include Margate bags, crocheted bucket hats and wraparound sunglasses. GRAZIA: What is a moment you will remember from the Burberry High Summer campaign shoot?  SIMONE ASHLEY: Our last day I was shooting on rock with Ryan (McGinley) by the water and we would climb, shoot and chat. That was fun. G: Where do you find your confidence from when wearing Burberry?   SA: It brings out my inner London girl, which I do think is the best. G: How does Burberry fit into your personal style in the summer?  SA: I always like to dress simple in the summer, so pairing my looks with a cool Burberry piece makes it feel a bit more elevated. G: What is your essential Burberry item in the summer and why?  SA: My Burberry handbag and if I’m in England, my wellies since you never know what British summer has to offer.   G: What about shooting the Burberry High Summer campaign captures the perfect British summer’s day?  SA: A little bit of hot sun and a little bit of wind. Disclaimer: This article offers general fashion and lifestyle inspiration. Individual results, preferences, and styling outcomes may vary. title: This Spider Launches at 130 G’s (Humans Black Out at 9) - A-Z Animals
content:
Some predators chase. Others ambush. And then there are the ones that shoot. Across the animal kingdom, a surprising number of species have evolved ways to hit prey from a distance — firing water jets, venom sprays, sticky silk, or even microscopic harpoons. These aren’t gimmicks. They’re highly specialized hunting systems designed to solve one core problem: how to catch prey that is faster than the predator. Many rely on what scientists call latch-mediated spring actuation (LaMSA) — a way to store energy and release it instantly, like a biological trigger. The result is explosive speed, extreme precision, and in some cases, forces that rival human-made machines. From fish that snipe insects out of the air to snails that fire venom-loaded harpoons, these predators have weaponized the laws of physics. While many creatures, such as bombardier beetles and spitting cobras, “shoot” to defend themselves, these elite marksmen use their abilities offensively, actively hunting and capturing their next meal. If you thought fish only hunted underwater, the archerfish will quickly prove you wrong. This aquatic outlaw can knock insects off branches up to 5 feet above the water by firing a precise jet of water from its mouth. But it’s not just about force or power; it’s about physics and precision. Because light bends when it passes from air to water, the fish’s view of the insect is distorted. To hit its mark, the archerfish must account for this “refraction” before firing. This skill isn’t purely instinctual, either. Young archerfish practice to refine their aim, eventually achieving near-perfect accuracy, especially when they position themselves directly beneath their target. While many spiders wait for prey to get stuck in their webs, the spitting spider actively immobilizes its prey. In a fraction of a second, the spider sprays a specialized mixture of liquid silk and venom from its fangs while it rapidly vibrates its head, causing the silky streams to form a zigzagging net in the air. This sticky, toxic mesh pins insects to whatever surface they’re on. It’s fast, precise, and extremely effective. Unlike other eight-eyed spider species, spitting spiders only have six eyes. While most shrimp are scavengers, the pistol shrimp is a gunslinger of the sea. Unlike its relatives, this impressive hunter doesn’t kill by pinching; it uses physics to hunt. When the shrimp slams its oversized claw shut, it ejects a high-speed jet of water that creates a cavitation bubble. This bubble is a tiny pocket of low pressure that collapses almost instantly, triggering a massive release of energy. The pistol shrimp may only be 1.5 inches long, but it can move its claws at 60 miles per hour. The collapse of this bubble is so violent that it produces a shockwave louder than a gunshot, stunning or killing small prey from a distance. For a tiny fraction of a second, the temperature inside the collapsing bubble can reach nearly 8,700 degrees Fahrenheit (4,800 degrees Celsius). The energy release is so intense it actually produces a brief flash of light. The shrimp’s claw features a unique spiral, crack-resistant structure designed to withstand the repeated stress of these underwater “explosions.” Materials scientists are actively studying the pistol shrimp’s claw to develop stronger, more impact-resistant armor and helmets for humans. The velvet worm may appear soft, slow, and relatively harmless, but it is a surprisingly effective predator. Using specialized nozzles near its mouth, it sprays twin jets of slime at a speed reaching nearly 5 feet per second. The slime hardens in the air as the jets oscillate rapidly, and the movement ensures that the slimy webbing covers a large area to make it nearly impossible for an insect to escape. Once its prey is immobilized, the worm dissolves the victim’s insides and consumes the liquefied remains. Velvet worms typically hunt woodlice, crickets, and spiders. The worm also eats the dried strands of slime afterward, ensuring that no resources are wasted after the energy-intensive hunt. Unlike other spiders that build stationary webs and wait for prey to get caught, the ogre-faced spider actively casts its net like a tiny eight-legged fisherman. Hanging upside down from a single silk line, the spider holds a small, rectangular frame of highly elastic silk between its front legs. It uses its specialized slit sensilla or vibration sensors to detect the movements of insects walking or flying nearby. When an insect comes within range, the spider lunges, stretching the net to several times its original size and snapping it over the prey. Two of the ogre-faced spider’s eyes face forward and are much bigger than the rest, giving it excellent low-light night vision. The cone snail moves at a literal snail’s pace, yet its strike is one of the fastest and most lethal in nature. Instead of chasing its prey, this snail uses its hollow, venom-tipped tooth—essentially a built-in biological harpoon—to strike with incredible speed. It happens in less than one millisecond, even faster than the blink of a human eye. Once the harpoon hits, the snail injects a complex cocktail of neurotoxins that shuts down the prey’s nervous system instantly. Some cone snail species can launch their harpoon in less than 100 microseconds. Some species, like the geography cone (Conus geographus), are so toxic that a single sting can be fatal to humans. This species is sometimes called the “cigarette snail” due to a grim myth: if you are stung, you’ll only have enough time to smoke one last cigarette before you die. While this may be an exaggeration, the venom does act extremely quickly. Instead of spinning traditional webs, the bolas spider uses a specialized “fishing” technique to catch its prey. Instead of a net, it creates a single silk line tipped with a heavy and sticky glob called a bolas. The bolas’ incredibly sticky texture allows it to bypass a moth’s slippery scales and adhere directly to its body. The bolas spider creates silk with a sticky texture. ©Judy Gallagher / CC BY 2.0 / Wikimedia Commons – Original / License The spider releases a scent that mimics the pheromones of a female moth. When a male moth approaches in search of a mate, the spider swings the bolas in a circular motion to snag the moth out of the air. The chameleon is a long-range predator that relies on mechanical advantage rather than raw muscle power to catch its prey. Its long tongue doesn’t simply reach out to snatch up an insect; instead, the chameleon launches it from its mouth like a high-speed projectile. This mechanism relies on stored energy rather than rapid muscle movement, allowing it to function even in cooler temperatures. Chameleons have a unique physiology that allows them to “wind up” energy in their tongue’s tissue so they can snap it out at speeds of 60 miles per hour in a fraction of a second. The tip of the chameleon’s tongue has a suction cup-like pouch coated with saliva that is about 400 times thicker than human saliva. This special coating ensures that the bond is strong enough to pull the prey back, even if it is heavy or struggling. Rather than waiting for an insect to fly into its web, the slingshot spider transforms its entire web into a loaded spring. It constructs a conical web and pulls the center tight using a tension line attached to a nearby surface. By pulling back and holding the web under extreme tension, the spider stores potential energy, essentially turning itself into a living bow and arrow. When the spider senses prey, it releases the tension line, sending itself and its web catapulting forward at over 130 Gs (for perspective, humans typically black out at around 9 Gs). The sudden launch collapses the web over the prey, entangling it before it can react. Slingshot spiders shoot their own bodies at their prey. ©LOPEZ André / CC BY-SA 4.0 / Wikimedia Commons – Original / License The same silk structures that provide the tension also act as shock absorbers, bringing the spider to a safe stop at the end of its flight. title: Becky G’s Blinding Christian Cowan Top Is See-Through With Side Cutouts - theFashionSpot
content: Becky G, who received Billboard’s 2026 Latin Women in Music Global Impact Award, posed in a blinding, see-through Christian Cowan cutout top in a new photoshoot for the magazine. On April 23, the “Shower” singer attended the Billboard Latin Women in Music Gala in Miami in a sheer Roberto Cavalli dress. For the new Billboard photoshoot, Becky G picked out an outfit straight from the runway. The ensemble was Look 14 from the Christian Cowan Fall 2026 Ready-to-Wear Collection, presented at the New York Fashion Week. The top featured a sleeveless, relaxed silhouette, side cutouts, and a classic crew neckline. The metallic mesh fabric created a liquid-like drape that caught the light with an intense shimmer. Underneath the top, she wore a black Fleur du Mal bra, which added depth and an edgy, layered appeal. The lingerie piece provided a dark, matte foundation that peeks through the semi-translucent metal mesh. The bottom half of the ensemble grounded the ethereal sparkle of the top with sharp tailoring. The black Christian Cowan shorts featured structured pleats and a wide waistband with prominent belt loops. By wearing the shorts low on her hips and posing with her top lifted slightly, Becky G created a bold, midriff-baring silhouette. The heavy, tailored shorts contrasted against the delicate, shimmering top, balancing the hard and soft fashion elements. She wore her black hair straight with a center part. The singer’s long hair reached past her waist with a high-gloss finish that mirrored the sheen of the top. Further, her makeup was warm and luminous, featuring a soft brown smoky eye, groomed brows, and a nude lip gloss. At the Billboard Latin Women in Music Gala, Becky G walked the red carpet in a gold Roberto Cavalli dress from the Spring/Summer 2026 collection. She paired the high-slit gown with gold platform stilettos featuring delicate ankle straps. Ankita Shaw is a Lifestyle writer at Evolve Media. She creates engaging content for fashion, beauty, and pop culture beats. Her passion for storytelling helps her bring fresh perspective to her writing.

title: Becky G’s See-Through Roberto Cavalli Dress Has a Huge Cutout & High Slit - theFashionSpot
content: Becky G stunned at the Billboard Latin Women in Music Gala 2026 in a see-through Roberto Cavalli dress featuring a high slit and a massive cutout in the torso. She delivered a masterclass in shimmering, statuesque glamour on the red carpet, pairing the floor-length gown with gold-toned shoes. Photos and videos of her red carpet and her stage looks have taken over the internet. At the Billboard Latin Women in Music Gala 2026 on April 23, Becky G turned heads in an outfit straight off the runway. Crafted from a ribbed, metallic fabric, the see-through dress was Look 37 from Roberto Cavalli’s Spring/Summer 2026 collection titled “Gold Obsession” presented at the Milan Fashion Week. The dress featured a form-fitting silhouette with a high halter neckline. It also had a dramatic asymmetrical cutout that swept across the torso, exposing her midriff and hip. A metallic serpentine brooch sat at the waist, adding a touch of primal elegance to the sophisticated fabric. The gown’s pleated skirt fell to the floor with a thigh-high slit. The slit broke up the monotonous gold color scheme with a glimpse of her bare leg. The combination of the sheer illusion pleats and the structured cutouts created a look that was both ethereal and daring. Becky G paired the dress with towering gold platform stilettos with thin ankle straps. The “Shower” singer wore her long black hair in glossy, voluminous waves with a middle part, cascading down her shoulders. Meanwhile, her makeup featured a warm bronze eyeshadow, a sharp winged liner, and a matte nude lip. The soft glam approach enhanced her natural features without overpowering the dress. Dainty gold earrings completed her look. Further, Becky G took the stage in a sparkling sheer dress from Manuri Spring/Summer 2026 collection. She accessorized with a matching sequined headwrap low across her forehead. Ankita Shaw is a Lifestyle writer at Evolve Media. She creates engaging content for fashion, beauty, and pop culture beats. title: How does the BMW F 450 GS compare to its rivals? - BBC TopGear India
content:
The BMW F 450 GS is the latest ADV to join India's highly competitive Adventure bike segment. Launched at ₹4.70 lakh and going all the way up to ₹5.30 lakh, it is on the higher end of the ADV spectrum. Does it have what it takes to take the crown off the single-cylinder motorcycles, like the KTM Adventures and the Royal Enfield Himalayan? Let's find out. The BMW F 450 GS is powered by a 420cc parallel twin engine with a unique 135-degree crankshaft. It produces 47.3bhp at 8,750rpm and 43Nm of torque at 6,750rpm and is mated to a six-speed gearbox. BMW offers a quickshifter as standard in all variants except the base, and the GS trophy variant gets BMW's Easy Ride Clutch (ERC), which eliminates the need to use the clutch below 2700rpm in stop-and-go traffic and even while starting or slowing down. The BMW F 450 GS is equipped with fully adjustable KYB USD fork up front and an adjustable KYB monoshock at the rear. On the braking front, it has Brembo callipers with BMW branding. The front gets a 310mm disc with a 4 piston caliper and a 240mm disc at the rear with a single piston floating caliper. Its rivals, like the KTM Adventure, get adjustable forks from WP front and rear, the Royal Enfield Himalayan and the Honda NX 500 get non-adjustable Showa suspension, and the Kawasaki Versys X gets basic telescopic forks up front and a monoshock at the rear. The BMW F 450 GS rides on a 19-inch wheel up front and a 17-inch wheel at the rear and comes equipped with knobby Maxxis tyres. Rivals like the Honda NX 500, KTM Adventure 390 X and the Kawasaki Versys-X 300 also come with a 19-inch front and a 17-inch rear setup, while the Himalayan and the KTM Adventure S get a more contemporary 21-inch front and a 17-inch rear. The KTM also offers the Adventure R model with an 18-inch rear wheel, which is a more off-road-focused offering. The BMW F 450 GS is one of the lighter motorcycles in its segment, weighing in at just 178kg with a fuel tank capacity of 14 litres. It has a seat height of 845mm, which can be adjusted to 830mm with a lowered seat and increased to 865mm with an optional rally seat. The BMW F 450 GS is one of the best-equipped motorcycles in its segment. It gets a large 6.5-inch TFT display with 4 riding modes: Rain, Road, Enduro and Enduro Pro. It gets a quickshifter on the exclusive and the GS trophy and the GS Trophy also gets the easy ride clutch. It has traction control, dual-channel ABS and heated grips but misses out on cruise control. Rivals like the KTM Adventure series offer similar equipment to the BMW, with a TFT display, ride modes, quickshifter, ABS, and traction control, but it gets cruise control as standard on all models, which is a big plus. The Himalayan and the Honda NX 500 offer similar equipment, with a TFT display, ride modes, ABS, but miss out on a quickshifter and cruise control. One thing to note is that the Himalayan does not come with traction control in any of its variants. The most basic of the bunch is the Kawasaki Versys-X 300, which gets a basic semi-digital instrument cluster with just dual-channel ABS. Prices for the BMW F 450 GS start at ₹4.70 lakh for the base model and go up to ₹5.30 lakh for the top GS Trophy variant. It is one of the most expensive middleweight ADVs in India, with only the Honda NX 500 being more expensive, while the single-cylinder bikes are around a lakh cheaper. Here are the prices of all the bikes. In conclusion, the F 450 GS is a well-equipped bike with a strong engine, but demands a premium over the KTMs and Royal Enfeilds. But it is more value for money compared to the Kawasaki Versys-X 300 and the Honda NX 500. This website is owned and operated by Exhibit Technologies PVT LTD. title: BMW F 450 GS first ride review - BBC TopGear India
content:
Price, ERC clutch should be optional on the top-end mode This one has been a long time coming. I’ve finally gotten my hands on the BMW F 450 GS, and honestly, it isn’t quite the bike I expected it to be. In some ways, it has impressed me more than I thought it would; in others, it has left me wanting a little more. But before I get into all of that, here are my first impressions of BMW Motorrad’s all-new adventure tourer.The F 450 GS — the ‘baby GS’ — absolutely nails its biggest trump card: presence. It looks fantastic. Borrowing heavily from the design language of the R 1300 GS, it genuinely feels like a scaled-down version of its bigger sibling from every angle. It’s sharp, purposeful and, despite being smaller in person than pictures suggest, it still carries a wonderfully imposing road presence. It has that unmistakable GS stance — the kind that makes people stop and take a second look.What really sets it apart, though, is that it manages to do something none of its ADV-touring rivals quite manage — it feels genuinely premium. The fit and finish are excellent, from the quality of the paintwork to the consistently tight panel gaps. The switchgear feels tactile and expensive, the bodywork feels solid, and thoughtful use of materials like magnesium alloy for the clutch and alternator covers, a hollow aluminium swingarm, plus golden USD forks only adds to that upmarket feel. It feels every bit like a BMW should — and it better, because prices start at ₹4.7 lakh, ex-showroom.There are three variants on offer — Base, Exclusive and GS Trophy — priced at ₹4.7 lakh, ₹4.9 lakh and ₹5.3 lakh, respectively. What’s the difference between the three, you ask? Well, the Base model gets LED lighting all around, a Bluetooth-enabled 6.5-inch TFT screen, cornering ABS, traction control, three ride modes, adjustable levers, heated grips, a Type-C charging port and those rather lovely golden USD front forks.Step up to the Exclusive variant, and it adds enduro foot pegs, black hand guards, an engine guard, an additional riding mode called Enduro Pro, a clear windscreen and a quickshifter. Then there’s the full-fat GS Trophy variant, which gets adjustable KYB suspension at both ends, BMW’s Easy Ride Clutch tech — more on that later — an aluminium engine guard, white hand guards, a white main frame and a tinted windscreen. The first two variants are available only in Cosmic Black, while the GS Trophy comes dressed in BMW’s signature Racing Blue Metallic shade. The bike only misses out on cruise control, which would’ve been a neat and helpful addition.We expected prices to hover around the ₹5 lakh mark, and that’s exactly where this bike lands. Yes, it is pricier than most of its rivals, with only the Honda NX500 sitting around a lakh higher — and even that doesn’t feel as well-equipped or as premium. The F 450 GS has a lot going on, and once you dig deeper, the pricing — although introductory and likely to go up soon — doesn’t seem unreasonable at all.The use of all those premium materials has also helped keep weight impressively low. At 178kg wet — the engine weighs only 46kg — for an adventure tourer with a parallel-twin engine and this much kit on board, that’s a seriously impressive number. And you feel it immediately. The GS feels light from the moment you take it off the stand. It’s easy to manoeuvre, feels wonderfully nimble, and never has that intimidating top-heavy feel larger ADVs often do.Now to the meat of the matter — the riding experience. I rode the top-spec GS Trophy variant, which comes equipped with the Easy Ride Clutch, and it made for a very interesting first ride. The standard seat height is 845mm, which could feel a bit tall for shorter riders. At 5’7”, I couldn’t quite flat-foot it completely, but because the bike is so light and manageable, it never felt like a real issue. That said, off-road, especially on uneven terrain or awkward inclines, shorter riders may have to work a little harder. The rider’s triangle is fairly neutral, with an ever-so-slightly forward-set but tall handlebar and forward-set footpegs.Powering the baby GS is a 420cc liquid-cooled parallel-twin engine producing 47.3bhp at 8,750rpm and 43Nm of torque at 6,750rpm. The power figures give it the best-in-class power-to-weight ratio, and BMW claims that 80 per cent of the total torque is available from just 3,000rpm — and you can feel that the moment you open the throttle. It launches hard, has a strong and usable mid-range, and while it doesn’t chase the redline with the same manic urgency as a KTM single, it gathers speed quickly and feels properly quick once you’re higher up the rev range.I was, however, expecting the engine to be a little more refined. It does feel slightly vibey. The vibrations begin to build once you start pushing harder and become quite noticeable past the 4,500rpm mark, especially through the tank and footpegs. It also doesn’t sound quite as exciting as you’d hope. There isn’t much of that deep, bassy note you’d expect from a twin-cylinder motor, and overall, it feels a little less characterful in the sound department than anticipated. Both aren’t deal-breakers at all, though, and it is definitely more refined than the single-cylinder engines that almost all of its rivals are equipped with. Throttle calibration is spot on, and the way the power is delivered is also butter smooth.The mill is mated to a six-speed gearbox, which gets a slipper clutch and a quickshifter on the two top variants. But more importantly, the GS Trophy variant gets the Easy Ride Clutch. This is essentially a centrifugal clutch which automatically engages and disengages, removing the need for you to use the clutch lever at all — save for when you have to start the bike. Think of it as a clutchless manual setup — you still shift through the gears yourself, but the bike handles all the clutch operation for you.This system has its share of pros and cons — and with that, I think it should’ve been optional, not standard on the top-end variant. Starting with the pros: it makes riding the bike the easiest affair — as simple as a conventional scooter with a CVT — and you get used to it very quickly. That, coupled with the quickshifter, means you barely need to use the clutch at all on this bike. The system comes into play after 2,700rpm, and that’s when the bike begins to roll, which means the gearbox can feel and sound a little like a CVT when you’re crawling through bumper-to-bumper traffic in first gear. It allows you to come to a complete standstill without having to pull the clutch, and while that feels slightly counterintuitive on a motorcycle, it genuinely makes life much easier in the city.You do, however, need to be in the right gear when setting off. Yes, the bike will pull away from a standstill even in higher gears, but this is still a mechanical system, and doing that repeatedly will only strain it unnecessarily and wear the clutch faster. That said, you can still use the lever normally and even pop wheelies like you would on a traditional manual bike. The quickshifter itself is excellent too — smooth, precise and properly seamless. Together, the whole setup makes the F 450 GS incredibly easy to ride, both on and off the road, and the best part is that you simply cannot stall it.One thing that does stand out as a drawback is how the bike behaves on an incline. Even if it’s left in gear with the engine switched off, it can still roll backwards, which means you have to be quite mindful while parking on slopes or uneven ground. It becomes even more noticeable off-road — if you stop halfway up a steep climb, managing the bike can get a bit awkward since there’s nothing helping you hold position. A simple parking brake or a hill-hold function would have been genuinely useful here.Where the bike truly shines, though, is in the way it handles and rides. It runs on a 19-inch front wheel and a 17-inch rear, both wrapped in Maxxis Maxxplore all-terrain tyres. Suspension duties on the GS Trophy variant are handled by 43mm golden upside-down front forks, adjustable for compression and rebound, while the rear monoshock gets adjustable damping. Both ends offer 180mm of travel.In the stock settings, the front end absorbs bumps really well, while the rear feels just a touch firm. It isn’t quite as plush as a Himalayan 450 — or even an Apache RTX — but it still does a commendable job of smoothing out broken roads. The upside is that this setup makes the bike feel excellent on smooth roads and surprisingly capable in corners, adding confidence and agility to the handling.With 220mm of ground clearance and neutral ergonomics that make standing up on the pegs easy, it feels very natural to ride off the tarmac as well. In short, the baby GS handles rough terrain well, although it isn’t as hardcore or dirt-focused as some of its rivals. The trade-off, however, is a motorcycle that feels easier, more comfortable and far more enjoyable to live with on the road — which, for many riders, may matter a lot more.The BMW F 450 GS feels like a motorcycle that has been designed with a very clear brief — to make adventure touring approachable, premium and genuinely easy to live with. And for the most part, it succeeds brilliantly. It looks like a proper GS, carries the road presence to match and, more importantly, feels every bit like a BMW should. The quality, the finish and the overall sense of solidity give it an edge that very few rivals in this segment can match.It is also surprisingly easy to ride. The low weight, friendly ergonomics and manageable power delivery make it far less intimidating than many middleweight ADVs tend to be. The Easy Ride Clutch, while not without its compromises, makes everyday commuting and even light off-roading feel almost effortless. Add to that strong highway manners, excellent handling and enough capability to tackle rough roads without complaint, and you have a very well-rounded package.Yes, I do wish the engine felt a little more refined and had a bit more character, and features like cruise control or hill-hold would have made an already strong package even better. I’d also like to spend more time with the manual clutch version, because I suspect that might offer a slightly sharper and more engaging riding experience for enthusiasts who prefer a more traditional setup.As for the pricing, ₹4.7 lakh to ₹5.3 lakh, ex-showroom, does feel expensive at first glance — especially when most of its rivals undercut it quite comfortably. But once you spend time with the bike, the premium starts to make sense. The quality of materials, the finish, the technology, the parallel-twin engine and the overall polish give it a sense of occasion that cheaper alternatives simply don’t. It feels like a motorcycle built to justify that badge on the tank. And that, perhaps, is the bigger point: the F 450 GS is not trying to be the cheapest option in the segment — it is trying to be the most complete one.But even with those caveats, the bigger picture remains clear — the F 450 GS gets the fundamentals absolutely right. It strikes a smart balance between everyday usability and genuine adventure capability, without becoming too extreme in either direction. It focuses on being the motorcycle you would actually want to ride every single day. And perhaps that is its biggest strength. It makes the idea of owning a GS feel more accessible, less intimidating and far more realistic for a wider audience. It may wear the ‘baby GS’ tag, but out on the road, it feels every bit like it belongs in the family. This website is owned and operated by Exhibit Technologies PVT LTD. title: GS E&C wins Seongsu 1 project, lifts Korea urban renewal orders past ₩4T - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
content: GS Engineering & Construction was selected as the Sigongsa for the redevelopment of Seongsu Strategic Maintenance Zone District 1 (Seongsu District 1) in Seongdong-gu, Seoul, pushing its cumulative urban maintenance project orders this year past 4 trillion won. According to the maintenance industry and GS Engineering & Construction on the 27th, the Seongsu District 1 redevelopment association held a regular general meeting on the 25th and passed an agenda item selecting GS Engineering & Construction as the Sigongsa. The Seongsu District 1 redevelopment is a project to build apartment complexes and ancillary and welfare facilities—17 buildings with 3,014 households, from four basement levels to up to 69 aboveground floors—across 194,398 square meters in Seongsu-dong 1-ga. The construction cost is 2.154 trillion won, the largest among the four districts within the Seongsu Strategic Maintenance Zone. GS Engineering & Construction proposed "RIVENIQUE XI" as the complex name to the association. GS Engineering & Construction's cumulative orders this year for urban maintenance projects total 4.0259 trillion won, which is about half of this year's order target of 8 trillion won. The company secured construction rights for the Songpa Hanyang 2nd reconstruction in Songpa-dong, Songpa-gu (685.6 billion won), and the Gaepo Woosung 6th reconstruction in Gaepo-dong, Gangnam-gu (215.4 billion won). title: Becky G’s Amiri Micro Minidress Is as See-Through as It Gets for Billboard - theFashionSpot
content: Becky G is stepping into a defining new era, and her latest ‘Billboard’ feature captures that shift with clarity. The shoot feels intentional and self-assured, reflecting an artist who is no longer experimenting but fully owning her identity. There is a quiet confidence in how she presents herself. One that aligns perfectly with the narrative she shares in her interview about growth, return, and creative freedom. As the visuals unfold, her styling makes a bold statement. Becky G wears a daring see-through Amiri micro minidress that plays with texture and transparency, creating a look that is both edgy and refined. The silhouette is structured by a cinched belt, adding shape and contrast to the fabric’s fluidity. Its ultra-short hemline emphasizes the legs, while the delicate, almost netted texture gives the piece a soft shimmer. She pairs the outfit with knee-high black leather boots from Thursday Boot Company, which add a grounded, slightly Western-inspired feel to the ensemble. Melinda Maria jewelry subtly elevates the look, adding just enough shine without overpowering the overall aesthetic. Her glam leans into a soft yet defined finish, with glowing skin, subtly contoured cheeks, and a glossy nude lip that keeps the look polished. The outfit mirrors the essence of her current artistic phase: bold, unapologetic, and deeply rooted in who she is. In her ‘Billboard’ interview, Becky G opens up about returning to her roots, describing it as a “full circle” moment. Having first entered the industry as a rapper, she now revisits that foundation with clarity and purpose. She also speaks about embracing her identity as a Mexican-American artist, explaining how her music and creativity now reflect both sides of her culture equally, as recently seen at Coachella. Overall, this feature is more than just a fashion moment; it reflects her evolution. Viha Shah is a Fashion Writer at Evolve Media, where she covers celebrity fashion, pop culture, and lifestyle trends. She closely follows red carpet style, street style, and the evolving fashion narratives shaped by public figures. With a keen eye for detail, she enjoys decoding celebrity looks, highlighting emerging trends, and exploring how fashion influences popular culture.

title: Interested in BMW F 450 GS? Here are its variants explained - HT Auto
content: Copyright © HT Media Limited  All rights reserved. The BMW F 450 GS has been introduced in India as the brand’s new entry point into the GS adventure lineup. It is offered in three variants, each catering to different types of riders with a gradual increase in equipment and capability. Here’s a clear breakdown of how they differ. Priced at around ₹4.70 lakh (ex-showroom), the base version is far from basic. It comes equipped with a 6.5-inch TFT display paired with BMW’s rotary controller for navigating menus. Features like heated grips stand out in this segment, along with essentials such as ABS, traction control, and adjustable brake and clutch levers. It also gets engine drag torque control and a height-adjustable rear brake lever, making it a fairly comprehensive package for everyday use as well as light touring. The mid-spec Exclusive trim, priced at roughly ₹4.90 lakh, builds on the base model with additional hardware aimed at improving touring and mild off-road capability. It adds protective components such as an engine guard made up plastic and knuckle guards, along with a taller windscreen for better wind protection. The inclusion of Riding Modes Pro and Shift Assistant Pro enhances the riding experience, especially on longer journeys. Off-road-focused footpegs also make this version more suitable for trail use. At the top sits the GS Trophy variant, priced at about ₹5.30 lakh. This version gets all the features of the Exclusive trim and adds more specialised equipment. Key highlights include adjustable sport suspension and the Easy Ride Clutch system, which is designed to simplify riding in tricky conditions. It also comes with an aluminium engine guard and a rally-style tinted windscreen. Visually, it stands out with a distinctive tricolour paint scheme and white-finished elements that align with BMW’s GS Trophy identity. There is also Sport Suspension on offer which gives full adjustability to the rider. Across the range, the differences are mainly in added features rather than core mechanical changes. All three variants share the same platform and engine, but each step up introduces more touring comfort, electronic aids, and off-road readiness. In simple terms, the base model suits riders looking for a well-rounded entry ADV, the Exclusive adds practicality and tech, while the GS Trophy is geared towards people who want that exclusive colour scheme and Easy Ride Clutch (ERC).

title: GS E&C cements data center, smart city plots in Vietnam - The Korea Times
content: GS E&C President and CEO Huh Yoon-hong, front row left, poses with FPT Corp. CEO Nguyen Van Khoa, front row right, in Hanoi, Vietnam, Wednesday, after signing a memorandum of understanding to cooperate in developing and building data centers and smart cities. Courtesy of GS E&C Major engineering company GS E&C has secured footholds for expanding its data center and smart city projects in Vietnam on the back of President Lee Jae Myung’s state visit to the country last week. The construction arm of GS Group on Wednesday met representatives of FPT Corp., the largest private IT company in Vietnam and the country’s data center market leader by volume. The meeting in Hanoi brought together GS E&C President and CEO Huh Yoon-hong and FPT CEO Nguyen Van Khoa. The two signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) on data center development and smart city construction in Vietnam. Drawing on the Korean firm’s more than 20 years of experience in the data center business, the companies agreed to cooperate to support Vietnam’s digital economy growth and nationwide digital transformation. GS E&C said the data centers will be built in key regions across Vietnam with an initial capacity of tens of megawatts each. The facilities’ efficiency will be secured through modular construction. The two companies will also cooperate on introducing smart city infrastructure features based on artificial intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, developing plans for advanced traffic systems, energy monitoring systems and public safety platforms. Under the partnership, GS E&C will provide design and construction expertise for building AI infrastructure in Vietnam. FPT, meanwhile, will provide technological cooperation based on its expertise in cloud services and digital platforms, generate local demand and support partnerships with the Vietnamese government and local authorities. On Thursday, GS E&C signed another MOU on comprehensive financial services cooperation with the Bank for Investment and Development of Vietnam, Vietnam’s largest state-owned commercial bank. The agreement aims to establish a financial support framework for development projects and broader smart city businesses being pursued by VGSE, GS E&C’s Vietnamese subsidiary. Under the partnership, the two parties will provide various financial solutions, including project finance, guarantees and cash management, and introduce housing finance programs tailored to residents of future smart cities in Vietnam. “We will concentrate the company’s technological capabilities and infrastructure expertise to strengthen economic cooperation between Korea and Vietnam and contribute to Vietnam’s digital economic growth. We aim to leap forward from a mere construction company into a leading partner in smart, innovative technologies," a GS E&C official said. title: Naver Adds Fuel Rewards to Membership With GS Caltex Partnership - thelec.net
content: Naver has added a fuel rewards benefit to its subscription service, Naver Plus Membership, through a partnership with GS Caltex. Naver Plus Membership offers benefits including up to 5% in Naver Pay points on shopping and reservation payments, as well as monthly content subscriptions such as Netflix and Spotify, for a monthly fee of 4,900 won. Under the new partnership, members can earn 100 won in Naver Pay points per liter when refueling at GS Caltex stations nationwide, with a monthly cap of 5,000 won. To use the service, users must link their Naver Plus Membership once through the GS Caltex Energy Plus app. Points are then automatically accrued when paying with Naver Pay points or money via QR code at GS Caltex stations. Naver said the partnership expands its mobility-related ecosystem, following earlier collaborations with Uber, Socar and Aero K Airlines since January. The company also introduced Naver Pay QR functionality to its Membership Pass service, allowing users to manage and use multiple partner memberships in one place, improving payment convenience. Jeong Hanna, head of Naver Membership, said the partnership aims to expand everyday benefits for users as rising fuel prices increase cost burdens, adding that the company will continue to broaden both online and offline offerings to position the membership as an essential lifestyle service.

title: First Female Volleyball Player Ahn Hye-jin Gets Severe Warning, 5M Fine - 조선일보
content: KOVO cites low alcohol level, remorse in disciplining drunk-driving champion setter The disciplinary results for Ahn Hye-jin, who caused controversy by becoming the first female volleyball player to be caught driving under the influence, have been announced. The Korea Volleyball Federation (KOVO) stated, "On the morning of the 27th, the federation held a disciplinary committee meeting regarding Ahn Hye-jin's drunk driving incident in the conference room." Ahn Hye-jin was caught driving under the influence with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.032%, which is at the level of license suspension, on the morning of the 16th. The federation explained, "On the 17th, we confirmed the details of Ahn Hye-jin's drunk driving incident from the GS Caltex team and held today's disciplinary committee meeting to give Ahn Hye-jin an opportunity to state her case." The disciplinary committee members listened to Ahn Hye-jin's statement and explanation. They confirmed that there was a violation of the Road Traffic Act (drunk driving), reviewed and discussed the details, and decided on a severe warning and a penalty of 5,000,000 Korean won. The federation explained, "The disciplinary committee acknowledged that violating the Road Traffic Act (drunk driving) is a serious antisocial act and should be severely punished. However, considering factors such as ▲the relatively low blood alcohol concentration of 0.032%, ▲the fact that she immediately self-reported to the team and federation after the incident, ▲her deep remorse and reflection, ▲the fact that she effectively received a one-year suspension due to failing to secure a free agent contract, and ▲her exclusion from the national team, the committee decided, based on Article 10, Paragraph 1, Item 1 of the Disciplinary Regulations and Article 11, Paragraph 4 of the General Disciplinary and Penalty Imposition Standards (Appendix 1), to issue a 'severe warning and a penalty of 5,000,000 Korean won' to Ahn Hye-jin." Ahn Hye-jin, who graduated from Gangneung Girls' High School and was selected third in the first round of the 2016-2017 rookie draft, played as the starting setter for GS Caltex during the 2025-2026 season, leading the team to a championship finals victory after five years. GS Caltex finished third in the regular league, advanced through the semifinals and playoffs, and achieved a miracle by defeating the first-place Korea Expressway Corporation 3-0 in the championship finals, with Ahn Hye-jin at the center of it all. After the season, Ahn Hye-jin obtained free agent status. A blockbuster contract was anticipated as she earned the title of championship-winning setter, but she committed an irreversible mistake and kicked away her own good fortune. Ahn Hye-jin was caught driving under the influence by the police on the 16th and underwent investigation. The next day, she posted a handwritten apology on her personal SNS. GS Caltex also issued an official apology for the incident and bowed to volleyball fans. Ahn Hye-jin became the first female player in the history of the V-League women's division to be caught driving under the influence, earning her infamy. Ahn Hye-jin failed to secure a free agent contract during this offseason, making her ineligible to play in the 2026-2027 season. It is reported that she did not receive any contract offers from any team, including her original team GS Caltex.

title: Latin superstar Karol G's global tour travels to Arlington this fall - AOL.com
content: Colombian singer Karol G, fresh off a headlining performance at Coachella, will capitalize on that exposure with a world tour that will come to AT&T Stadium in Arlington on Thursday, October 15. Starting on July 24 in Chicago, Illinois, the “Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour" - which literally means "traveling around the world" - will stop in 39 cities around the globe over the course of exactly one year, ending in Milan, Italy on July 24, 2027. In addition to Arlington, Karol G will play Texas shows in San Antonio on September 2, El Paso on September 6, and Houston on September 27. The tour will see the singer become the first Latina artist to headline stadiums across Europe as part of a global tour. The first Latina artist to headline Coachella, Karol G has slowly but surely made her way into superstar status. After appearing on the Colombian music competition show El Factor X at age 14, she released multiple songs independently before putting out her debut album, Unstoppable, in 2017. That album went to No. 2 on Billboard's Top Latin Albums chart, and each of her subsequent four albums has gone to No. 2 or No. 1 there. Each of her last two albums, most recently Tropicoqueta in 2025, made the top 3 on the overall Billboard 200. Karol G has also collaborated with many other big Latin stars, including fellow Colombian Shakira, Becky G, Peso Pluma, Anuel AA, J Balvin, and more. Tickets for the tour, promoted by Live Nation, will be available starting on Monday, April 27 through various presales. To be eligible for the artist presale in the USA, Canada, and Europe, fans must register at karolgmusic.com by Friday, April 24 at 9 am. General on-sale timing will vary by market. Fans should check karolgmusic.com for specific details regarding their city. title: GS Group drives AI adoption to boost competitiveness and grow Korea’s ecosystem - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
content: GS Group is stepping up future innovation management based on digital and eco-friendly initiatives. The strategy is to enhance competitiveness by integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into traditional businesses and to help expand Korea's AI ecosystem. On the 27th, GS Group said the company holds a Silicon Valley-style idea competition, the "GS Group Hackathon," every year to drive groupwide AI innovation. The hackathon is a venue where all affiliates' employees use AI technology to solve challenges they face in the field, playing a key role in increasing on-the-job AI adoption and spreading a culture of innovation. The 4th GS Group Hackathon, held in Sep. last year, ran under the theme "PLAI: Play with GenAI." A record 837 people (256 teams), including group employees and outside organizations, participated. Over one night and two days, participants carried out the entire process from defining problems and proposing solutions to building service prototypes based on Generative AI. This hackathon introduced GS's self-developed AX (AI transformation) platform "MISO" and Vercel's vibe-coding tool "v0." At the competition, a variety of ideas were proposed, from work-efficiency improvements to ways to enhance customer experience at each business site, including gas stations, convenience stores and construction sites. Selected outstanding teams will move to real-world implementation with group-level support. Affiliates are also continuing AI-driven innovation. GS Caltex is pursuing "Digital & AI Transformation (DAX)," which integrates digital transformation (DX) and AI transformation (AX), to secure competitive advantage in the AI era. GS Caltex develops its own AI models based on massive data generated at the Yeosu plant and uses them to optimize production processes. It is also deploying smart safety equipment, including AI CCTVs, at business sites to detect potential hazards in real time. GS Engineering & Construction is fostering an in-house culture in which all employees use AI in their work. To communicate smoothly with foreign workers on site, it developed the AI translation program "Xi Voice," and, to prevent various errors when reviewing structural design documents, it introduced an AI-based "design drawing review system" in the field and completed a patent application in Aug. last year. GS Retail is pursuing field-centered AX. It created "VOC reconstruction," which uses Generative AI to identify improvement tasks from customer feedback by channel, and a "franchisee information-sharing system redesign," which can deliver tailored information to convenience store operators. GS25 uses AI in store operations and management. In product ordering, it runs an automatic ordering system based on AI Machine Learning. In store management, an AI service called "Aiari (Gen AI)" helps sales managers instantly check job manuals and practical materials in the field. GS Shop is using AI production technology not only for broadcast screens but also for producing promotional videos and fashion broadcast content. It plans to further strengthen its competitiveness in using AI data and improve partner sales performance. It will also provide AI practical-use training to all employees and reflect field feedback. In addition, GS E&R commercialized a "wind power generation forecasting solution" using AI Machine Learning techniques, becoming the first in the industry to reduce wind power generation forecast error rates to below 10%. GS Global is pursuing AX activities to minimize legal risks by automating repetitive contract comparison tasks with AI. GS Group also operates an innovation community, "52g" (5pen 2nnovation GS), which leads digital transformation (DX) to pursue innovation in how people work. The hallmark of 52g is discovering and sharing creative ideas through voluntary cooperation among members. At the senior management level, the "AI digital council," which includes all presidents within GS Group, is held every quarter. Huh Tae-soo, chairman of GS Group, said, "GS does not directly make products like AI Semiconductor, but we treat data as an asset, manage it properly and combine it with AI to create new value," adding, "We will boldly seize opportunities amid change so that GS and Korea's AI ecosystem can grow together."
